 {"id":"caam-1xv","title":"Research and implement Claude setup-token for headless auth","description":"# Research and Implement Claude Headless Auth

## Research Findings (Completed)

### Available Methods

#### 1. setup-token + CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN (Primary)
```bash
# Generate token
claude setup-token
# Returns: your-oauth-token-here

# Use in headless environment  
export CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN="your-oauth-token-here"
claude -p "your prompt"
```

**Known Issue**: GitHub #8938 reports the token doesn't fully bypass the startup interactive flow. User still sees theme/auth prompts even with token set.

#### 2. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (API Key Mode)
```bash
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-..."
claude -p "your prompt"
```
Works for print mode (-p), may not work for interactive sessions.

#### 3. Credential File Transfer (Workaround)
Transfer ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json to remote machine:
```bash
scp ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json user@server:~/.config/claude-code/auth.json
```

#### 4. SSH Port Forwarding (Workaround)
```bash
# Local machine
ssh -L 8080:localhost:8080 user@remote-server
# On remote, run claude /login, open the localhost URL locally
```

### Current Status in Claude Code

- No native device code flow (like codex --device-auth)
- setup-token exists but has bugs (#8938)
- Credential transfer is the most reliable workaround
- GitHub #7100 requests proper headless auth documentation

### Implementation Path for caam

1. **AuthModeSetupToken**: New auth mode using CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
2. **Token Import**: Command to import setup-token result
3. **Credential Export/Import**: Enhanced backup/restore for headless transfer

### Files to Modify

- internal/provider/claude/claude.go
- Add setupToken flow that:
  a. Checks for CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env var
  b. If not set, prompts user to run \`claude setup-token\` elsewhere
  c. Stores token in profile for future use

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Document current limitations clearly
- [ ] Implement token-based auth if feasible
- [ ] Fallback: Provide clear instructions for credential transfer via caam backup/restore

### References

- https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/8938
- https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/7100

 {"id":"caam-234","title":"Usage Analytics","description":"# Usage Analytics

## Purpose
Track profile usage activity to give users visibility into their patterns and help identify underutilized or overutilized profiles.

## Background (from codex-pool)
codex-pool tracks detailed usage with BoltDB:
- Per-request logs (debugging)
- Per-account aggregates (dashboard)
- Automatic pruning (retention policy)

## Adaptation for caam
caam does not proxy requests, so we cannot track API usage directly. Instead, we track **caam activity**:
- Profile activations
- Login/refresh events
- Errors encountered
- Session durations

## What This Epic Delivers
1. SQLite database setup and schema
2. Activity logging API
3. Session duration tracking
4. Usage aggregation queries
5. CLI usage command
6. TUI usage panel (optional)

## Why SQLite (not BoltDB)
- Query flexibility (complex aggregations)
- Familiar tooling (sqlite3 CLI, DB browsers)
- Mature ecosystem
- CGO-free drivers available (modernc.org/sqlite)

## Database Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE activity_log (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,
    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    provider TEXT NOT NULL,
    profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    details TEXT,
    duration_seconds INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE profile_stats (
    provider TEXT NOT NULL,
    profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    total_activations INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    total_errors INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    total_active_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    last_activated DATETIME,
    last_error DATETIME,
    PRIMARY KEY (provider, profile_name)
);
```

## Event Types
- `activate` - Profile activated
- `deactivate` - Profile deactivated (implicit when switching)
- `login` - User logged in
- `refresh` - Token refreshed
- `error` - Error occurred
- `switch` - Switched from one profile to another

## Retention Policy
- Keep detailed logs for 30 days (configurable)
- Keep aggregates indefinitely
- Auto-prune on startup

## User Experience
```bash
$ caam usage
Profile Usage (last 7 days)
───────────────────────────
claude/work         42 sessions   18.5 hrs
claude/personal     12 sessions    4.2 hrs
codex/main          28 sessions   12.1 hrs

$ caam usage --profile claude/work
Session History for claude/work (last 7 days)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
2025-12-17 09:15  2.3 hrs  (active)
2025-12-16 14:20  1.8 hrs
2025-12-16 09:00  3.1 hrs
```

## Dependencies
- None (parallel to health features)

## Future Enhancements
- Export to CSV/JSON
- Integration with provider usage APIs
- Cost tracking (if providers expose this)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:16:31.226242657-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:44:47.338639408-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:44:47.338639408-05:00","close_reason":"Fully implemented: SQLite db package (db.go, migrations.go), activity logging (events.go), session tracking (session.go), usage CLI command with table/json/csv output.  {"id":"caam-2bm","title":"Multi-Machine Vault Sync","description":"# Epic: Multi-Machine Vault Sync

## Overview
Enable intelligent synchronization of vault data across multiple machines, automatically pushing fresh auth tokens to all machines in a sync pool.

## Background
Power users have multiple machines (work laptop, home desktop, cloud VM). When a token is refreshed on one machine, they want it available on all machines without manual copying. This is the ultimate "set and forget" workflow.

## User Story
As a user with multiple machines, I want my auth tokens to automatically sync across all machines, so that I always have the freshest credentials available everywhere.

## Design Philosophy
- **Push-based, not polling**: When auth is refreshed/backed up, immediately push to other machines
- **Freshness wins**: Always prefer the token with the latest expiry time
- **Union merge**: Profiles are merged across machines (A has alice+bob, B has alice+carol → both get alice+bob+carol)
- **Graceful degradation**: Offline machines get synced when they come back online
- **SSH transport**: Leverage existing SSH infrastructure for secure, authenticated transport

## Machine Discovery

### 1. SSH Config Parsing
Parse `~/.ssh/config` to auto-discover hosts:
```
Host work-laptop
    HostName 192.168.1.100
    User jeff
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/work_key
```

### 2. User CSV File
Create/maintain `~/caam_sync_list.csv`:
```csv
machine_name,ip_address,path_to_ssh_key_file
work-laptop,192.168.1.100,~/.ssh/id_ed25519
home-desktop,10.0.0.50,~/.ssh/home_key
cloud-vm,34.123.45.67,~/.ssh/cloud_rsa
```

### 3. TUI Management
- View sync pool in TUI
- Add/edit/remove machines
- Test connectivity
- View sync status per machine

## Sync Protocol

### Freshness Determination
OAuth tokens contain expiry information. Compare:
1. `expires_at` / `expires_in` from token JSON
2. File modification time as tiebreaker
3. Deterministic: later expiry always wins

### Sync Algorithm
```
On local auth change (backup/refresh/activate):
  1. For each machine in sync pool:
     a. SSH connect (with retry)
     b. Read remote token for same provider/profile
     c. Compare freshness (local vs remote)
     d. If local newer → push to remote
     e. If remote newer → pull to local (unexpected but handle it)
     f. Log sync action
  2. Queue failed machines for retry
  3. Update last_sync timestamp
```

### Triggering
- **Auto-trigger**: After backup, refresh, or activate (if sync enabled)
- **Manual**: `caam sync` command
- **Periodic retry**: Process queue on any caam command

## CLI Commands
```bash
caam sync                     # Sync now with all machines
caam sync status              # Show sync pool status
caam sync add <machine>       # Add machine to pool
caam sync remove <machine>    # Remove machine from pool
caam sync test <machine>      # Test SSH connectivity
caam sync enable              # Enable auto-sync
caam sync disable             # Disable auto-sync
caam sync log                 # Show sync history
```

## TUI Integration
- New panel/view for sync status
- Machine list with status indicators (🟢 synced, 🟡 pending, 🔴 error)
- Add/remove/edit machines
- Manual sync button
- Last sync timestamp per machine

## CSV File Auto-Creation
On first sync-related command, if `~/caam_sync_list.csv` doesn't exist:
1. Create file with header row
2. Optionally pre-populate from ~/.ssh/config
3. Print helpful message about editing the file

## Sync State Storage
`~/.local/share/caam/sync/`
```
sync/
├── pool.json           # Machine definitions and status
├── queue.json          # Pending syncs (for retry)
├── history.json        # Sync log (last N syncs)
└── state/              # Per-machine state
    ├── work-laptop.json
    └── home-desktop.json
```

## Edge Cases

### Machine Offline
- Queue the sync operation
- Retry on next caam command or sync
- Mark machine as "pending" in status

### Conflicting Updates
- Rare: same profile refreshed on two machines simultaneously
- Resolution: compare expires_at, later wins
- If exactly equal: prefer local (no network needed)

### New Machine Joins
- Full sync on first connect
- Pull all profiles from existing pool
- Push all local profiles

### Machine Removed
- Just remove from pool
- No data deletion on remote or local

### First Sync Ever
- If pool is empty, nothing to do
- If pool has machines, do full bidirectional sync

## Security Considerations
- All transport over SSH (encrypted)
- SSH keys must be properly secured
- No password storage - uses existing SSH key auth
- Warn user if CSV file has loose permissions
- Consider: sign sync data to prevent tampering?

## Requirements
- SSH client installed (standard on macOS/Linux)
- SSH key authentication configured
- Network access between machines (may need VPN for remote)
- Same caam version on all machines (or compatible)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parse ~/.ssh/config for host discovery
- [ ] Create/read ~/caam_sync_list.csv
- [ ] SSH connectivity test per machine
- [ ] Freshness comparison using token expiry
- [ ] Push newer tokens to remote machines
- [ ] Pull newer tokens from remote machines
- [ ] Queue and retry for offline machines
- [ ] Auto-sync on backup/refresh (when enabled)
- [ ] Manual sync command
- [ ] TUI sync status view
- [ ] TUI machine management
- [ ] Sync history logging

## Dependencies
- Requires Export/Import Bundle epic for bundle format knowledge (shared serialization)

## Effort Estimate
Large - new subsystem with networking, state management, error handling

## Future Enhancements (Out of Scope)
- Cloud-based sync (Dropbox, Google Drive integration)
- Web-based sync dashboard
- Mobile notification when tokens sync

## Context
Expose sync functionality in the TUI for users who prefer the graphical interface. This includes viewing sync status, managing machines, and triggering syncs.

## TUI Changes

### New Panel: Sync Status
Add a new panel/view accessible via 'S' key:

```
┌─ Sync Pool ─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Status: Enabled (auto-sync on)                              │
│ Last sync: 5 minutes ago                                    │
│                                                             │
│ Machines:                                                   │
│   🟢 work-laptop     192.168.1.100     synced 5m ago       │
│   🟢 home-desktop    10.0.0.50         synced 5m ago       │
│   🔴 cloud-vm        34.123.45.67      offline (2h)        │
│                                                             │
│ Pending: 0 | Failed: 1 | Queue: 1 item                     │
│                                                             │
│ [a]dd  [r]emove  [t]est  [s]ync now  [e]dit CSV  [q]uit   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Status Indicators
- 🟢 Online, synced
- 🟡 Online, pending sync
- 🔴 Offline or error
- 🔄 Currently syncing

### Keyboard Shortcuts (in sync view)
- 'a' - Add machine (opens dialog)
- 'r' - Remove selected machine (with confirmation)
- 't' - Test selected machine connectivity
- 's' - Sync now with all/selected machine
- 'e' - Edit CSV file in editor
- 'l' - View sync log
- 'Enter' - View machine details
- 'Esc/q' - Return to main view

### Add Machine Dialog
When 'a' is pressed:
```

│                                                             │

│                                                             │
│           [Add] [Test & Add] [Cancel]                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

```
│                                                             │
```
```
│                        [Cancel]                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Integration with Main View
In the main profile list, show sync status:
```
claude
  alice@gmail.com     active    🔄 syncing
  bob@gmail.com                 🟢 synced
  carol@gmail.com               ⚠️ pending
```

### Help Panel Update
Add sync shortcuts to help:
```
Sync:
  S       Open sync panel
  Ctrl+S  Sync now (from any view)
```

- `internal/tui/sync_view.go` - Sync panel view
- `internal/tui/sync_model.go` - Sync panel model
- `internal/tui/sync_update.go` - Sync panel update handlers

### Model Changes

```go
    // ... existing fields ...

}

    pool          *sync.SyncPool

}
```

### Key Handling

```go
case "S":
    return m.showSyncView()
    
case "ctrl+s":
    return m.triggerSyncNow()
```

### Sync Panel
```go
func (m SyncViewModel) View() string {
    // Render sync panel using lipgloss
    // - Header with status
    // - Machine list with status indicators
    // - Footer with keybindings
}

func (m SyncViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (SyncViewModel, tea.Cmd) {
    switch msg := msg.(type) {
    case tea.KeyMsg:
        switch msg.String() {
        case "a":
            return m.showAddMachineDialog()
        case "r":
            return m.confirmRemoveMachine()
        // etc
        }
    case SyncProgressMsg:
        // Update progress display
    case SyncCompleteMsg:
        // Show results, refresh
    }
}
```

4. **Runtime Enhancements** - Hot reload, signals

 {"id":"caam-3b3","title":"Uninstall command","description":"Provide a clean way to remove caam and restore original state.

## Command
```
caam uninstall [flags]

Flags:
  --restore       Restore _original profiles before removing (default: true)
  --keep-vault    Keep vault directory (default: false)
  --keep-config   Keep config files (default: false)
  --force         Skip confirmation prompt
  --dry-run       Show what would be done without doing it
```

## Default Behavior (Interactive)
```
$ caam uninstall

caam uninstall will:
  ✓ Restore original auth for: claude, codex (gemini has no _original)
  ✓ Remove vault directory (~/.local/share/caam/vault/)
    - 3 claude profiles
    - 2 codex profiles  
  ✓ Remove config (~/.config/caam/)
  ✓ Remove activity database (~/.local/share/caam/caam.db)

Proceed? [y/N] y

Restoring claude auth from _original_claude... done
Restoring codex auth from _original_codex... done
Removing vault... done
Removing config... done
Removing database... done

caam has been uninstalled. Your original auth state has been restored.
```

## Edge Cases
- No _original exists for a tool → skip restore for that tool, warn user
- Vault doesn't exist → skip vault removal
- Restore fails → abort uninstall, report error

## Implementation
1. Create cmd/caam/cmd/uninstall.go
2. Add restore logic (iterate _original_* profiles)
3. Add cleanup logic (remove directories)
4. Add dry-run support
5. Add confirmation prompt

Single command to completely reverse all caam changes and restore original state.

The uninstall MUST happen in this exact order:

 {"id":"caam-3ef","title":"Health status calculation logic","description":"# Health Status Calculation

## Purpose
Calculate profile health status (🟢🟡🔴) based on token expiry, errors, and penalties.

## Background (from codex-pool)
```go
score = headroom * planBonus * creditBonus - penalty
```

For caam v1, we simplify to status tiers with an internal numeric score.

## Technical Requirements

### Status Enum
```go
type HealthStatus int

const (
    StatusUnknown  HealthStatus = iota // ⚪ Cannot determine
    StatusHealthy                      // 🟢 Good to use
    StatusWarning                      // 🟡 Expiring soon or errors
    StatusCritical                     // 🔴 Expired or many errors
)

func (s HealthStatus) Icon() string
func (s HealthStatus) String() string
```

### Scoring Algorithm
```go
func CalculateHealth(h *ProfileHealth) (HealthStatus, float64) {
    score := 0.0
    now := time.Now()
    
    // Factor 1: Token expiry (primary)
    if h.TokenExpiresAt.IsZero() {
        // Unknown expiry - neutral
    } else if h.TokenExpiresAt.Before(now) {
        score -= 1.0  // Expired
    } else {
        ttl := h.TokenExpiresAt.Sub(now)
        switch {
        case ttl > time.Hour:
            score += 1.0
        case ttl > 15*time.Minute:
            score += 0.5
        default:
            // < 15 min: no bonus
        }
    }
    
    // Factor 2: Recent errors
    switch {
    case h.ErrorCount1h == 0:
        score += 0.3
    case h.ErrorCount1h <= 2:
        // Neutral
    default:
        score -= 0.5
    }
    
    // Factor 3: Plan type bonus
    switch h.PlanType {
    case "enterprise":
        score += 0.3
    case "pro", "team":
        score += 0.2
    }
    
    // Factor 4: Penalty (from errors, with decay)
    score -= h.Penalty
    
    // Convert to status
    status := StatusHealthy
    if score < 0 {
        status = StatusCritical
    } else if score < 0.5 {
        status = StatusWarning
    }
    
    return status, score
}
```

### Thresholds (Configurable)
```go
type HealthConfig struct {
    TokenExpiryWarningMinutes int     // Default: 60
    TokenExpiryCriticalMinutes int    // Default: 15
    ErrorCountWarning         int     // Default: 2
    ErrorCountCritical        int     // Default: 5
}
```

## User-Facing Display
```
🟢 Healthy   - Good to use
🟡 Warning   - Token expiring soon or recent errors
🔴 Critical  - Token expired or many errors
⚪ Unknown   - Cannot determine status
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HealthStatus enum with Icon() and String()
- [ ] CalculateHealth function with documented algorithm
- [ ] Configurable thresholds via HealthConfig
- [ ] Unit tests covering all status transitions
- [ ] Edge cases: zero time, negative penalty, nil health

- `internal/health/status_test.go` (new)

 {"id":"caam-9os","title":"OAuth refresh handler for Google Gemini","description":"# OAuth Refresh Handler for Google Gemini

## Purpose
Implement OAuth token refresh for Google Gemini using standard Google OAuth2 flow.

## Reference (from codex-pool)
```go
func (h *proxyHandler) refreshGeminiAccount(ctx context.Context, a *Account, 
    refreshTok string) error {
    form := url.Values{}
    form.Set("client_id", geminiOAuthClientID())
    form.Set("grant_type", "refresh_token")
    form.Set("refresh_token", refreshTok)
    // POST to https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token
}
```

**KEY DIFFERENCE**: Gemini uses form-encoded body, NOT JSON!

## Technical Requirements

### Constants
```go
const (
    GeminiTokenURL = "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"
)

// Client ID from gcloud ADC or gemini CLI
func geminiClientID() string {
    // This may need to be read from the ADC file
    // or use a known client ID
}
```

### Auth File Location
```
~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json
```

### ADC File Format
```json
{
  "client_id": "764086051850-6qr4p6gpi6hn506pt8ejuq83di341hur.apps.googleusercontent.com",
  "client_secret": "d-FL95Q19q7MQmFpd7hHD0Ty",
  "refresh_token": "1//...",
  "type": "authorized_user"
}
```

**Note**: ADC file contains client_id and client_secret - use these for refresh!

### Refresh Function
```go
func RefreshGeminiToken(ctx context.Context, clientID, clientSecret, refreshToken string) (*TokenResponse, error) {
    form := url.Values{}
    form.Set("client_id", clientID)
    form.Set("client_secret", clientSecret)
    form.Set("grant_type", "refresh_token")
    form.Set("refresh_token", refreshToken)
    
    req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "POST", GeminiTokenURL, 
        strings.NewReader(form.Encode()))
    req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded")
    
    resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("gemini refresh failed: %w", err)
    }
    defer resp.Body.Close()
    
    if resp.StatusCode != 200 {
        body, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("gemini refresh error %d: %s", 
            resp.StatusCode, string(body))
    }
    
    var tokenResp TokenResponse
    json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&tokenResp)
    
    return &tokenResp, nil
}
```

### Google TokenResponse
```go
// Google returns slightly different format
type GoogleTokenResponse struct {
    AccessToken  string `json:"access_token"`
    ExpiresIn    int    `json:"expires_in"`    // Seconds
    Scope        string `json:"scope"`
    TokenType    string `json:"token_type"`
    // Note: refresh_token is NOT returned on refresh
}
```

### File Update Approach
For Gemini/gcloud ADC, we typically do NOT update the ADC file. Instead:
1. Store the access token separately for caam use
2. Or update health metadata with expiry only
3. The ADC file is managed by gcloud CLI

```go
func UpdateGeminiHealth(provider, profile string, resp *TokenResponse) error {
    // Update health metadata only
    health := healthStore.GetProfile(provider, profile)
    health.TokenExpiresAt = time.Now().Add(
        time.Duration(resp.ExpiresIn) * time.Second)
    return healthStore.UpdateProfile(provider, profile, health)
}
```

## Alternative: Read-Only Approach
Since gcloud manages ADC, we might just:
1. Read expiry from access token (if JWT)
2. Parse token to check expiry
3. Recommend user run `gcloud auth login` if expired

- Health metadata storage (for updating expiry)

## Considerations
- Should we modify ADC file or just track expiry?
- Interaction with `gcloud auth` commands
- Multiple Google accounts scenario","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:20:37.044266582-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:38:54.28050005-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:38:54.28050005-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented OAuth refresh logic for Google Gemini. -{"id":"caam-9y2","title":"Export/Import Vault Bundle","description":"# Epic: Export/Import Vault Bundle

## Overview
Create a single-command export that bundles the entire vault into a portable zip file, and a corresponding import command to restore it on another machine.

## Background
Users with multiple machines need to transfer their auth profiles. Currently this requires manual copying of directories. A single zip bundle makes this trivial and ensures nothing is missed.

## User Story
As a user with multiple machines, I want to export all my saved auth profiles to a single file, so that I can easily set up caam on another machine with all my accounts.

## Filename Format
`Exported_Coding_Agent_Account_Auth_Info__As_of__MM_DD_YYYY__HH_MM_AM.zip`

Example: `Exported_Coding_Agent_Account_Auth_Info__As_of__12_19_2025__02_29_PM.zip`

## Bundle Contents
```
export_bundle/
├── manifest.json          # Export metadata, checksums, version
├── vault/                 # All provider profiles
│   ├── claude/
│   │   ├── alice@gmail.com/
│   │   └── bob@gmail.com/
│   ├── codex/
│   └── gemini/
├── config.yaml            # User configuration (optional)
├── projects.json          # Project associations (optional)
├── health.json            # Health metadata (optional)
└── caam.db                # Activity database (optional)
```

## Manifest Schema
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "caam_version": "1.2.3",
  "export_timestamp": "2025-12-19T14:29:00Z",
  "source_machine": "hostname",
  "source_platform": "darwin_arm64",
  "profiles": {
    "claude": ["alice@gmail.com", "bob@gmail.com"],
    "codex": ["work@company.com"],
    "gemini": []
  },
  "includes": {
    "config": true,
    "projects": true,
    "health": true,
    "database": false
  },
  "checksums": {
    "vault/claude/alice@gmail.com/.claude.json": "sha256:abc123..."
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
### Export
- [ ] `caam export [--output PATH]` creates zip bundle
- [ ] TUI export action (key 'E')
- [ ] Automatic filename with timestamp
- [ ] Includes vault, optionally config/projects/health/db
- [ ] Manifest with checksums for integrity verification
- [ ] Progress indicator for large exports
- [ ] Success message shows path to created file

### Import
- [ ] `caam import <bundle.zip>` restores from bundle
- [ ] TUI import action (key 'I')
- [ ] Validates manifest and checksums
- [ ] Handles version compatibility
- [ ] Merge mode: add profiles that don't exist, skip existing
- [ ] Replace mode: overwrite everything
- [ ] Preview mode: show what will be imported without doing it
- [ ] Handles cross-platform path differences

## Technical Notes
- Use Go's archive/zip for bundle creation
- Paths in zip must be relative, not absolute
- Cross-platform: handle ~/.local/share/caam vs %APPDATA%\caam
- Consider optional encryption (AES-256) for sensitive tokens?

## Security Considerations
- Bundle contains bearer tokens - warn user about securing the file
- Delete bundle after import? Or leave for user to manage
- Don't include SSH keys or other unrelated secrets

## Dependencies
None - standalone feature

## Effort Estimate
Medium - new subsystem but straightforward file I/O","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:38:09.146772172-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T14:38:09.146772172-05:00"}

#### 1. Device Code Flow (Recent Update - August 2025)
Gemini CLI now provides an auth URL to copy/paste, returning an activation code. This is similar to device code flow:
- CLI shows auth URL
- User opens URL on any device
- User gets code to enter back in CLI
- Works in headless environments!

**Status**: This appears to be a recent improvement. Need to verify current behavior.

#### 2. GEMINI_API_KEY Environment Variable
```bash
export GEMINI_API_KEY="your-api-key"
gemini "your prompt"
```
Direct API key authentication for Gemini API.

#### 3. GOOGLE_API_KEY Environment Variable
```bash
export GOOGLE_API_KEY="your-google-api-key"
gemini "your prompt"
```
Google Cloud API key authentication.

#### 4. Application Default Credentials (ADC)
```bash
export GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS="/path/to/service-account.json"
export GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT="your-project"
export GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION="us-central1"
gemini "your prompt"
```
For service account authentication - best for automation and CI/CD.

#### 5. BROWSER Workaround (Legacy)
Set BROWSER to a script that captures the auth URL:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "Auth URL: \$1" >> /tmp/gemini-auth-url.txt
```

Then manually complete auth on another device.

 {"id":"caam-fwz","title":"OAuth refresh handler for Claude Code","description":"# OAuth Refresh Handler for Claude Code

## Purpose
Implement OAuth token refresh for Claude Code to automatically renew tokens before expiry.

## Research Required
**IMPORTANT**: Claude Code OAuth implementation needs reverse engineering.

### Questions to Answer
1. Where is the oauth.json file located?
2. What is the token endpoint URL?

4. Is it JSON body or form-encoded?
5. What scopes are required?

### Likely Locations to Check
- `~/.claude/oauth.json`
- `~/.claude.ai/oauth.json`  
- `~/.config/claude/oauth.json`
- Check Claude Code source/docs if available

## Technical Requirements (Template)

### Refresh Function
```go
func RefreshClaudeToken(ctx context.Context, refreshToken string) (*TokenResponse, error) {
    // Build request
    body := map[string]string{
        "client_id":     claudeClientID,
        "grant_type":    "refresh_token",
        "refresh_token": refreshToken,
        // "scope":      "...",  // if needed
    }
    
    req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "POST", claudeTokenURL, ...)
    req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")  // or form-encoded
    
    // Execute
    resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("refresh request failed: %w", err)
    }
    defer resp.Body.Close()
    
    // Parse response
    if resp.StatusCode != 200 {
        // Handle error
    }
    
    var tokenResp TokenResponse
    json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&tokenResp)
    
    return &tokenResp, nil
}
```

### TokenResponse Structure
```go
type TokenResponse struct {
    AccessToken  string `json:"access_token"`
    RefreshToken string `json:"refresh_token,omitempty"` // May rotate
    ExpiresIn    int    `json:"expires_in"`              // Seconds
    ExpiresAt    string `json:"expires_at,omitempty"`    // ISO8601
    TokenType    string `json:"token_type"`
}
```

### File Update
```go
func UpdateClaudeAuth(path string, resp *TokenResponse) error {
    // Read existing file
    existing, _ := os.ReadFile(path)
    var auth map[string]any
    json.Unmarshal(existing, &auth)
    
    // Update fields
    auth["access_token"] = resp.AccessToken
    if resp.RefreshToken != "" {
        auth["refresh_token"] = resp.RefreshToken
    }
    auth["expires_at"] = resp.ExpiresAt
    // ... or calculate from expires_in
    
    // Atomic write
    return atomicWriteJSON(path, auth)
}
```

## Safety Requirements
1. **Never lose refresh token** - if refresh fails, keep original
2. **Atomic writes** - use temp file + rename
3. **Backup original** - keep .bak file before overwrite
4. **Verify new token** - optionally test before committing

## Error Handling
- Invalid refresh token → mark profile as "needs login"
- Network error → retry with backoff
- Rate limited → respect Retry-After header
- Unknown error → log and keep original token

 {"id":"caam-gir","title":"Runtime Enhancements","description":"# Runtime Enhancements

## Purpose
Improve runtime behavior with hot reload, file watching, and signal handling for a smoother user experience.

## Background (from codex-pool)
codex-pool supports hot reload without restart:
```go
POST /admin/reload → h.pool.replace(newAccounts)
```

## Why This Matters
Users often:
1. Run TUI in one terminal
2. Add/modify profiles in another terminal
3. Expect TUI to reflect changes

Without hot reload, users must restart TUI to see changes.

## What This Epic Delivers
1. File watching for profiles directory (fsnotify)
2. Automatic TUI refresh on changes
3. `caam profiles reload` CLI command
4. SIGHUP handling for daemon mode
5. Graceful reload (no state loss)

## Technical Approach

### File Watching
Use fsnotify to watch:
- `~/.caam/profiles/` directory
- Provider-specific auth directories (optional)

Events to handle:
- CREATE: New profile added
- MODIFY: Profile updated
- DELETE: Profile removed
- RENAME: Profile renamed

### TUI Integration
```go
func (m *Model) watchProfiles() {
    watcher, _ := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
    watcher.Add(profilesDir)
    
    for event := range watcher.Events {
        m.reloadProfiles()  // async refresh
    }
}
```

### Signal Handling
```go
sigChan := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
signal.Notify(sigChan, syscall.SIGHUP)

go func() {
    for range sigChan {
        reloadConfiguration()
    }
}()
```

## User Experience
```
# Terminal 1: TUI running
┌─ Claude Profiles ─────────┐
│ ● work@company.com   🟢   │
│   personal@gmail.com 🟡   │
└───────────────────────────┘

# Terminal 2: Add new profile
$ caam backup claude newproject@corp.com
Profile saved.

# Terminal 1: Auto-updates!
┌─ Claude Profiles ─────────┐
│ ● work@company.com   🟢   │
│   personal@gmail.com 🟡   │
│   newproject@corp.com 🟢  │  ← NEW
└───────────────────────────┘
```

## Edge Cases
- Rapid file changes: Debounce (100ms)
- Corrupted profile: Log warning, skip
- Permission denied: Log error, continue watching
- Watch limit exceeded: Fall back to polling

 {"id":"caam-hzu","title":"Add AuthModeDeviceCode to provider interface","description":"# Add AuthModeDeviceCode to Provider Interface

## What

Extend the provider.AuthMode enum to include device code flow as a first-class authentication method.

## Why

The OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant (RFC 8628) is fundamentally different from browser-based OAuth:
- No localhost redirect needed
- User authenticates on a separate device
- CLI polls for token completion
- Better UX for headless environments

This deserves its own AuthMode rather than being a flag on OAuth because:
1. Different flow mechanics (polling vs redirect)
2. Different timeout characteristics
3. Different user interaction model
4. Profiles may be configured specifically for device-code-only scenarios

## Implementation Details

### File: internal/provider/provider.go

Add new constant:
\`\`\`go
const (
    AuthModeOAuth      AuthMode = "oauth"       // Browser-based OAuth flow
    AuthModeAPIKey     AuthMode = "api-key"     // API key authentication
    AuthModeDeviceCode AuthMode = "device-code" // OAuth device code flow (RFC 8628)
    AuthModeVertexADC  AuthMode = "vertex-adc"  // Vertex AI ADC
)
\`\`\`

### Interface Extension

Consider adding optional interface for providers that support device code:
\`\`\`go
// DeviceCodeProvider extends Provider with device code flow support
type DeviceCodeProvider interface {
    Provider
    // SupportsDeviceCode returns true if this provider supports device code flow
    SupportsDeviceCode() bool
    // LoginWithDeviceCode initiates device code authentication
    LoginWithDeviceCode(ctx context.Context, p *profile.Profile) error
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AuthModeDeviceCode constant added
- [ ] DeviceCodeProvider interface defined (optional extension)
- [ ] Existing code continues to work (no breaking changes)
- [ ] Documentation comments explain when to use device-code vs oauth

## Testing

- Unit test that AuthModeDeviceCode is distinct from other modes
- Verify SupportedAuthModes() can include multiple modes
